title: Control label cardinality
description: Keep label value sets bounded; never label series with unbounded identifiers like user id or request id.
body: Use when defining metric labels or diagnosing TSDB memory pressure. Do not attach unbounded labels (user id, request id, full path) — each unique label combination is a new series and cardinality grows multiplicatively until the server degrades. Actions: enumerate expected label values, drop or aggregate high-cardinality dimensions at scrape or relabel time, and inspect series counts per metric. Validation signals include total active series staying within the tested budget and stable memory. Risk boundary: cardinality explosion degrades the whole Prometheus instance, not one query — treat an unbounded label as a blocking defect and escalate before it OOMs.
